question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the query exposes the full node_account_id (via coalesce) without any ownership verification, to identify the Hedera account IDs of all node operators, then use those account IDs to submit transactions that reference those accounts as signers without actually having the corresponding private keys?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endConsensusTimestamp field (ab.end_consensus_timestamp) to identify address book entries that have already expired (non-null end timestamp), then use the adminKey from an expired entry to attempt to authorize transactions during the period between the address book expiry and the network's recognition of the new address book?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the query performs no authentication or authorization check before returning adminKey data, to repeatedly poll the endpoint and detect changes in adminKey values over time, inferring when node key rotations occur and timing attacks to exploit the brief window when both old and new keys may be valid?
